articles.title: Creating a development sandbox with crosvm
articles.summary: Creating a development sandbox with crosvm allows developers to run sensitive code without compromising security. By using a separate computer for development, developers can minimize the risk of supply-chain attacks and protect their work from being compromised by malicious third-party dependencies. This approach enables developers to focus on testing and validation without worrying about the potential risks associated with running untrusted code.

articles.title: Avoiding Fallback in Distributed Systems
articles.summary: Distributed systems are becoming increasingly complex, with many components relying on each other to function. However, a common issue is the fallback mechanism that can occur when one component fails or becomes unavailable. This can lead to cascading failures and downtime for the entire system. To mitigate this risk, developers need to understand how to design and implement robust fallback mechanisms in distributed systems.

articles.title: Box3D, an open source 3D physics engine
articles.summary: Box3D is an open source 3D physics engine that extends the capabilities of Box2D with features such as triangle mesh collision and height-field collision, allowing for more realistic simulations in games. The core architecture remains similar to Box2D, but with additional functionality including sub-stepping solver, continuous collision, graph coloring, and large world support. This new engine is designed to be used by developers who need a robust physics solution for their 3D games, particularly those that require managing hundreds of thousands of entities on the server.

articles.title: Building a passive Ethernet tap
articles.summary: A passive Ethernet tap is a simple device that sits inline between your computer and router, copying signals onto two extra monitor ports without injecting traffic back into the network. The tap effectively bypasses power requirements and software configuration, making it a low-cost solution for monitoring or testing Ethernet connections.
